title: Spekta Behavior IR
description: Language-agnostic intermediate representation for test behavior hierarchies.

sources.description: Array of source files that were analyzed.

source.description: A single test source file and its top-level groups.

file.description: Path to the source file, relative to the project root.

type.description: The kind of spec this source file represents.

groups.description: Top-level group nodes in this source file.

groupNode.description: A group node representing a level in the test hierarchy (feature, describe, or context).

type.description: The kind of group: feature (top-level feature block), describe (subject or scope), or context (conditional branch).

children.description: Child nodes, which may be nested groups or example leaf nodes.

exampleNode.description: An example node representing an individual test case.

type.description: The kind of example: scenario (Capybara/feature spec style) or it (RSpec style).

description.description: Human-readable description of the expected behavior.

comments.description: Attribute comments attached to this example, providing metadata such as rationale.

steps.description: Ordered sequence of actions performed within this example.

comment.description: An attribute comment providing metadata about an example. Attribute semantics are defined in the IR spec, not in the analyzer.

attribute.description: The attribute name (e.g. 'why'). Defines the kind of metadata this comment carries.

text.description: The content of the attribute comment.

step.description: A single action or assertion within an example.

action.description: The type of action: visit (navigate to URL), click_on (click an element), fill_in (enter text into a field), select (choose from a dropdown), expect (assert a condition), or other (any action not covered by the above).

target.description: The target of the action (e.g. a URL path, button label, field name, or expected content).

value.description: Optional value associated with the action (e.g. the text to fill in, the option to select).
